gaps: No existing test covers the Software tab UI behavior for Charge Stand name/image when power state changes (unplug/plug).
No test validates that Charge Stand accessory metadata (name, image) persists correctly in the Software tab during power state transitions.
No test covers the lifecycle of a Charge Stand accessory display — specifically the unplugged-from-power state triggering a UI reset to placeholder values.
No test validates the re-population of Charge Stand name/image when power is restored (plugged back in).
No test covers the Software tab as a distinct UI surface for accessory (Charge Stand) display — all existing tests focus on the Device Home Screen or device list, not the Software tab.
No test covers power-state-dependent UI rendering for accessories (Charge Stand) — only headset power state (on/off) is tested, not accessory power (plugged/unplugged).

generated_testcases.title: TC-001: Charge Stand name and image persist in Software tab when unplugged from power
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the connected device (e.g., Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC).
Navigate to Device Settings → Software tab.
Verify the Charge Stand accessory is displayed with its correct name (e.g., 'Charge Stand') and correct image/icon.
Note the exact name and image shown.
Unplug the Charge Stand from power (remove USB-C cable or disconnect from power source).
Wait 5 seconds.
Observe the Software tab UI for the Charge Stand entry.
Verify the Charge Stand name and image remain unchanged from the noted values — they should NOT revert to a placeholder name (e.g., 'Accessory') or placeholder icon.
generated_testcases.expected: The Charge Stand name and image in the Software tab remain correct and unchanged after unplugging from power. No placeholder text or default icon replaces the accessory metadata.

generated_testcases.title: TC-002: Charge Stand name and image re-populate correctly in Software tab when plugged back into power
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the connected device → Device Settings → Software tab.
Verify the Charge Stand is displayed with correct name and image.
Unplug the Charge Stand from power.
Wait 5 seconds.
Observe the Software tab — note if the Charge Stand name/image changes to placeholder (e.g., 'Accessory' / generic icon).
Plug the Charge Stand back into power.
Wait 10 seconds for the accessory to re-initialize.
Observe the Software tab UI for the Charge Stand entry.
Verify the Charge Stand name and image return to the correct values (e.g., 'Charge Stand' with correct icon).
generated_testcases.expected: After reconnecting power, the Charge Stand name and image in the Software tab are restored to the correct, non-placeholder values. The UI does not remain stuck on a placeholder.

generated_testcases.title: TC-003: Charge Stand metadata remains stable in Software tab during rapid power toggle (unplug/plug cycle)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to Device Settings → Software tab.
Verify Charge Stand is displayed with correct name and image.
Rapidly unplug and re-plug the Charge Stand from power 3 times in succession (each unplug/plug cycle within 2 seconds).
After the third plug-in, wait 10 seconds.
Observe the Software tab UI.
Verify the Charge Stand name is still the correct name (e.g., 'Charge Stand'), not a placeholder.
Verify the Charge Stand image/icon is the correct accessory icon, not a generic placeholder.
generated_testcases.expected: After rapid power toggle cycles, the Charge Stand name and image remain correct in the Software tab. No placeholder values appear.

generated_testcases.title: TC-004: Charge Stand placeholder state does not persist after power restore and app restart
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to Device Settings → Software tab.
Verify Charge Stand is displayed with correct name and image.
Unplug the Charge Stand from power.
Wait 5 seconds.
Observe if the Charge Stand name/image changes to placeholder.
Plug the Charge Stand back into power.
Wait 10 seconds.
Fully close (kill) the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Reopen the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to Device Settings → Software tab.
Verify the Charge Stand is displayed with its correct name and image.
generated_testcases.expected: After power restore and app restart, the Charge Stand name and image in the Software tab are correct. No placeholder values persist across app sessions.

generated_testcases.title: TC-005: Charge Stand placeholder state does not persist after power restore and Bluetooth reconnect
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to Device Settings → Software tab.
Verify Charge Stand is displayed with correct name and image.
Unplug the Charge Stand from power.
Wait 5 seconds.
Observe if the Charge Stand name/image changes to placeholder.
Plug the Charge Stand back into power.
Wait 10 seconds.
Disconnect Bluetooth from the device (turn off Bluetooth or disconnect from app).
Reconnect Bluetooth to the device.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to Device Settings → Software tab.
Verify the Charge Stand is displayed with its correct name and image.
generated_testcases.expected: After power restore and Bluetooth reconnection, the Charge Stand name and image in the Software tab are correct. No placeholder values persist through the Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect cycle.

generated_testcases.title: TC-006: Charge Stand name and image remain correct in Software tab when navigating away and back after unplug
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to Device Settings → Software tab.
Verify Charge Stand is displayed with correct name and image.
Unplug the Charge Stand from power.
Wait 5 seconds.
Navigate away from the Software tab to another tab (e.g., Audio Settings or Home).
Wait 3 seconds.
Navigate back to Device Settings → Software tab.
Verify the Charge Stand name and image are correct and not placeholder values.
generated_testcases.expected: After unplugging from power and navigating away and back to the Software tab, the Charge Stand name and image remain correct. No placeholder values appear upon returning to the tab.

generated_testcases.title: TC-007: Charge Stand name and image remain correct in Software tab when app is backgrounded and restored after unplug
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to Device Settings → Software tab.
Verify Charge Stand is displayed with correct name and image.
Unplug the Charge Stand from power.
Wait 5 seconds.
Press the Home button to background the app.
Wait 10 seconds.
Reopen the Poly Studio Mobile app from the recent apps list.
Observe the Software tab UI.
Verify the Charge Stand name and image are correct and not placeholder values.
generated_testcases.expected: After unplugging from power and backgrounding/restoring the app, the Charge Stand name and image in the Software tab remain correct. No placeholder values appear.

generated_testcases.title: TC-008: Charge Stand placeholder does not appear in Software tab when Charge Stand is unplugged but device remains connected via Bluetooth
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to Device Settings → Software tab.
Verify Charge Stand is displayed with correct name and image.
Verify the headset/earbud is connected via Bluetooth and the app shows the device as connected.
Unplug the Charge Stand from power while keeping the headset/earbud connected via Bluetooth.
Wait 10 seconds.
Observe the Software tab UI for the Charge Stand entry.
Verify the Charge Stand name is still the correct name (e.g., 'Charge Stand').
Verify the Charge Stand image/icon is the correct accessory icon.
generated_testcases.expected: Even when the Charge Stand is unplugged from power, as long as the headset remains connected via Bluetooth, the Charge Stand name and image in the Software tab remain correct. No placeholder values appear.
